feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: What are the ABAP release + SP requirements for a connection to S/4HANA 2023 Java ADS?
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I was looking for some details about the software requirements to connect an ABAP system (NetWeaver + S/4) to an S/4HANA 2023 Java ADS (Adobe Document Services). But I couldn't find any detailed information in the Guides, Notes or Help Portal documentation.

I'm pretty sure there had been special prerequisites in the past... but I haven't been able to find them again... 🙈

Does anyone here know the requirements regarding ABAP release and SP level and can provide the corresponding source?

Thanks & regards, Marie

entry.title: Customer->BP Synchronization Error(CVI)
entry.content:
I am doing ECC to S/4HANA Conversion.

I ran customer synchronization(T-code 'CVI_COCKPIT' > 'CVI load' Tab > Load Tool), some of which caused errors.

The error message is as follows.

Message no. AM328 'You cannot create a comment with serial number 000'

When I analyzed it, when KNA1-KUNNR = BUT020-PARTNER, it could be KNA1-ADRNR ≠ BUT020-ADDRNUMBER.

However, when looking up KNA1-ADRNR and BUT020-ADDRNUMBER in the ADRC table, the values of the two data must match, and the error seems to occur if the two data are inconsistent.

If you know how to make the error disappear when I perform Customer->BP Synchronization, please share it.

I don't want a way to modify the data one by one.

Thank you.

entry.title: Data Service (OData Service - it has Virtual Element) - into SAC using SAC live connection.
entry.content: Hi all, can we able to access - OData Service (OData Service - it has Virtual Element) - into SAC using SAC live connection.

entry.title: Entire Asset value adjustment against GL account
entry.content:
Hello SAP Team, 

 

We recently migrated from ECC to S/4 Sep-24 and performed Asset Value adjustment project. 

 

After this activity business received audit feedback for around DIGITS approx. assets that these should not be created as assets in books.   Now we need to adjust these asset value against GL DIGITS balance. 

 

 

 

Thanks & Regards

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: I would like to know how to obtain SOC1 and SOC2 reports (steps)
entry.content:
Dear Support staff:

I don't know how to obtain SOC1 and SOC2 reports (steps),
so please tell me how to obtain SOC1 and SOC2 reports (steps).

I look forward to hearing from you.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Adobe Document Services future Strategy
entry.content:
We know that ADS on-premise support will be ended at 2027.

What alternatives does SAP offer for on-premise ADS?
Will there be a new on-premise system to replace ADS, or will only cloud-based solutions be available?

We found a link about on-premise dedicator : https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/maintenance-strategy-adobe-forms-on-premise/ba-p/13627957
"As announced at the DSAG Technology Days 2024 in Hamburg, SAP HANA Extended Application Services, Advanced Model (XSA) will be the successor platform for Adobe Document Services."
Could you verify this statement for this is valid in currently?

"SAP plans to deliver this option during the support time frame of the S/4HANA 2025 release."
Could you send me more details and information about it? For example S/4HANA 2025 will containe the XSA with ADS functionality? How can we imagine the new on-premise architecture?

Thanks in advance

entry.title: How to start a career as a SAP Functional Consultant?
entry.content:
Hello fellow SAP community.

I just finished my master's degree in business and product management. During my studies, I found a keen interest in SAP, specially in S/4HANA.

Because of this, I am aspiring on becoming a functional consultant. To do so, I currently find myself doing the learning journey " Explore Integrated Business Processes in SAP S/4HANA", plus an institutional overview course on SAP S/4HANA. My goal is to take the SAP Certified Associate - Business Process Integration with SAP S/4HANA.

Does this certificate covers the requirements to start a career as a functional business processes consultant in SAP S/4HANA? What else should I consider to start my career as a SAP S/4HANA consultant?

I appreciate in advance the support and guidance you may provide.

Best regards,

Erick Dos Santos.

entry.title: Advanced Inter company sales and Advanced Customer returns
entry.content:
Hello,

Can someone advice what is the total number of days and resources required required to design, configure and unit test , quality test the following two items . If someone pls can give answer based on certain assumptions that will be also fine.

1) Advanced inter company sales

2) Advanced customer returns.

rgds

sar

entry.title: Manage Components and Services F6178 app availability
entry.content:
Is the Manage Components and Services F6178 app only available on Public Cloud? According to the fiori apps library page it is https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F6178')/S34. Same information is also on the Maintenance Backlog Overview F5105 app's fiori library page, F6178 is on the Related Apps tab only if you select the public cloud version.

However, if you select the private cloud/on-premise version of F5105 and go to app documentation, the F6178 is mentioned as a navigation target https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/e72f747389b340229f7fa343975bfa57/2bde7398f9074619a9ffb467c13785f4.html

entry.title: White Column in List View of My Fiori App
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I'm having a problem with my Fiori app. In the list view, there's always a white column on the right that I can't remove. I've tried various layout options, but nothing has helped so far.

 

 

Does anyone have any ideas on how to solve this problem? Thanks in advance!

 

SAP S/4HANA SAP Fiori tools SAP Fiori SAP Fiori Elements

entry.title: Migrate your data for make to order sales orders
entry.content:
We have activated the Migrate you data Fiori app to migrate Open Sales orders.  We need to be able to pass the configured options for a make to order material.  How do we upload the options (characteristics) in the templated.  


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Extracting S/4HANA ACDOCA into different BW/4HANA Models (AP, AR; AA; PCA, CCA)
entry.content:
Hi,

we are in the migration to of SAP ECC to S/4HANA (Rise) and are facing challenges specifically in FICO Area.

We are investigating extraction from ACDOCA using ACDOCA based extractors e.g. 0FI_ACDOCA_10 but we don't know how to identify the records to tie them to our existing data models in BW/4HANA (e.g. we have modles for AP, AR, CCA , PCA ) based on extractors like 0FI_AP_4, 0FI_AR_4, 0EC_PCA_3, 0CO_OM_CCA_1 and others,...). For CO-PA we currently use 0FI_ACDOCA_10 and filter on object type = EO (market segment) and validate the data but in generel we do not exactly know how to identify the records and feed our existing data models per module in BW/4.

How can we detect which data belongs to each module? Is there a field  (like item category or object type) that can be used to spot which data belongs where? 

We could not find any meaningful BW/4 S/4 related business content either.

Regards,

Andreas Fuchs

entry.title: Billing documents for clearing GLs
entry.content:
In 'Clearing between Company Codes - transaction code BUV' instead of customer and vendor, we are going to use Clearing GLs. 

I would like to know how we can create automated billing documents for the items in those clearing GLs?

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud -Master Form Template changes not showing up in the related Content Forms
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (TEST) - The updates and changes I've made in my Master Form Templates continuously are not showing up in the related content forms. 

 

For example, our company created a customer master form during original implementation that had no footer. No this is something we very much want to utilize. I identified the correct master form, downloaded it, updated it in Adobe LiveCycle, uploaded it. My assumption is now when I download the related content form it should should display this footer but it doesn't. Am i missing something? perhaps there is an additional step?

Similarly, we don't want any "Test Copy" watermarks in any of our forms in any situation. So I updated several master forms to delete the watermark object and binding. I than did the same process as above, but again none of the content forms show these changes. 

Please help! We really want to bring these changes into production. 

Thank you!

entry.title: Planned Independent Requirements reduction
entry.content:
Dear Gurus

Here is the context of my concern...
I've got one Sales order type that reduces Planned Independent Requirements.
I've got another Sales order type that doesn't reduce Planned Independent Requirements.

I'm trying to understand the settings that triggers PIR reducing.

I've investigated all the parameters listed below and aligned the second Sales order with the first one… in vain.

Do you have any idea of what I did wrong or what I didn't look at?

Thank you for your help.

MM03: View MRP1 > Strategy group

SPRO:

Sales and Distribution > Basic Functions > Availability Check and Transfer of Requirements > Transfer of Requirements > Classes, Types
Sales and Distribution > Sales > Sales Documents > Schedule lines

entry.title: Why Businesses Must Move to S/4HANA Before 2027: Risks and Opportunities
entry.content:
A Strategic Business Transformation, Not Just a 2027 Deadline

In today’s rapidly evolving business landscape, AI, real-time insights, and automation are no longer optional—they define competitive leadership. The future of intelligent enterprises will be shaped by digital agility, cost optimization, and risk resilience—and SAP S/4HANA is at the core of this transformation.

While SAP’s 2027 deadline for ECC support is a pressing concern, the real imperative is not just compliance—but seizing the opportunity for radical business improvement. Delaying migration isn’t just an IT issue; it is a strategic decision with profound financial, operational, and competitive consequences.

Organizations that act now can unlock the full potential of S/4HANA’s intelligent ERP capabilities, including:

I.AI-powered process automation to eliminate inefficiencies across finance, supply chain, and procurement.

II. Predictive analytics & real-time insights for data-driven decision-making at scale.

III. Cloud-enabled scalability & digital transformation for future-proofed business operations.

IV. Built-in compliance & risk controls to ensure adherence to evolving global regulations.

For industries such as manufacturing, retail, banking, life sciences, and energy, the shift to S/4HANA is not just a technology upgrade—it is a business reinvention. Companies that migrate now will gain a competitive edge in an AI-driven economy, where agility and automation define winners.

The cost of inaction is too high. Businesses that wait until the last moment will face soaring migration costs, talent shortages, and missed AI-driven efficiencies.

Why This is More Than an IT Decision

While many view SAP’s 2027 deadline as a technical milestone, it is far more than just an IT constraint. It represents a strategic business inflection point—one that separates industry leaders from laggards in the AI-powered digital era. The transition to S/4HANA is about:

i. Future-proofing business models in a digital-first, real-time economy.

ii.  Enabling real-time decision-making & automation to outpace competitors.

iii.  Driving operational excellence through AI, analytics, and process optimization.

The choice is clear: Migrate early and lead—or delay and risk being left behind.

2027: A Business Deadline, Not Just an IT Constraint

Many businesses mistakenly perceive the end of ECC support as a technical event—when in reality, it is a fundamental shift in SAP’s innovation roadmap.

By 2026-27, SAP will focus 100% of its new innovations—including AI-driven process automation, machine learning, and predictive analytics—exclusively on S/4HANA. Companies that remain on ECC will be locked out of next-generation capabilities, while competitors unlock new efficiencies in finance, supply chain, and operations.

The Hidden Risks of Delaying S/4HANA Migration -

1. Higher Migration Costs & Talent Shortages

· SAP migration expertise is already in high demand, and consulting costs will increase by 30-50% in 2026-27 (Gartner ERP Migration Cost Benchmark 2024).

· Fewer ECC-trained professionals will be available, making it expensive to maintain legacy systems.

· Last-minute migrations increase failure rates due to compressed project timelines and rushed decision-making

· Legacy Infrastructure & Integration Costs: Maintaining ECC on-premise infrastructure comes with rising costs for hardware, database licenses, and third-party integrations. SAP’s AI, BTP, and Industry Cloud solutions are optimized for S/4HANA, and running or integrating them on/with ECC creates additional integration complexity and costs.

2. Security & Compliance Risks: The Hidden Exposure

Cybersecurity and regulatory compliance are becoming top priorities for enterprises, yet legacy ERP systems like SAP ECC are increasingly vulnerable to security threats and compliance failures. According to Gartner’s 2024 IT Security Report, 65% of cyberattacks now target outdated ERP systems, and businesses running ECC beyond 2027 face a heightened risk of ransomware attacks due to the lack of security patches. Similarly, Accenture’s 2023 ERP Security Report revealed that 60% of ransomware attacks on ERP systems targeted legacy SAP ECC, with an average financial impact of $4.5M per incident.

Industries that are most affected by these risks are Financial Services, Life Sciences, and Manufacturing and they will struggle to maintain compliance and data integrity. The key challenges for organizations here will be -

·End of Security Patches: SAP will stop issuing critical security updates for ECC post-2027, making legacy systems highly vulnerable to cyber threats. Enterprises running ECC beyond the deadline will face increased risks of data breaches and ransomware attacks

·Regulatory Non-Compliance: Many new and evolving global compliance regulations—such as IFRS, ESG reporting, tax digitalization, and industry-specific mandates will be difficult, if not impossible, to implement in ECC. This creates compliance bottlenecks and exposes organizations to regulatory fines.

·Data Integrity & Governance Risks: Without real-time financial reconciliations and automated compliance capabilities (e.g., real-time tax reporting, digital audit trails), businesses risk inaccuracies in financial statements, delays in reporting, and potential legal repercussions.

3. The Competitive Innovation Gap

By 2025-26, AI-powered ERP systems will define industry leaders, enabling organizations to optimize finance, supply chain, and analytics with real-time intelligence. Companies that delay migration to S/4HANA risk falling behind competitors who leverage AI-driven automation, predictive analytics, and industry-specific innovations. The key advantages of S/4HANA that ECC-based organizations will miss out on include:

·Competitors leveraging real-time SAP AI insights will gain cost and operational efficiencies that ECC-based organizations cannot match.

·Siloed & Batch-Based Processing: In a world of real-time digital enterprises, batch-based processing in ECC creates data lags, slows decision-making, and limits automation. Businesses delaying migration will struggle to keep up with agile competitors leveraging real-time enterprise capabilities.

· Limited Industry-Specific Innovations: SAP continues to enhance industry-specific solutions (SAP IBP, SAP Ariba, SAP SuccessFactors, SAP BTP), which require S/4HANA as a foundational platform. ECC users will find it increasingly difficult to adopt innovations and industry-specific functionalities.

4.    Integration Challenges & Business Process Fragmentation

Seamless integration is the backbone of modern, AI-driven enterprises, and existing SAP ECC will struggle to keep pace with evolving digital ecosystems. As SAP continues to prioritize S/4HANA, businesses relying on ECC will face increasing challenges in connecting with cloud applications, AI platforms, and multi-cloud environments.

·SAP’s Future Roadmap is S/4HANA-First: The entire SAP ecosystem—including AI-driven applications, SAP Business AI, SAP BTP, SAP Sustainability Control Tower, and Industry Cloud—is built on S/4HANA. ECC users will face growing incompatibility issues with SAP and third-party applications.

·Higher API & Middleware Costs: ECC systems require extensive middleware and custom integrations to connect with cloud applications, IoT, AI, and analytics platforms. S/4HANA reduces this dependency by offering native API-based integrations, reducing middleware costs.

·Multi-Cloud & Digital Transformation Roadblocks: Many businesses are adopting hybrid and multi-cloud strategies. S/4HANA is designed for seamless cloud interoperability, whereas ECC requires additional investments in integration architect

5.    Last-Minute Migration Risks

As the 2027 deadline approaches, the surge in demand for SAP migration expertise will drive up costs, extend project timelines, and increase business disruptions. Organizations that delay transition will face:

·Higher Consulting & Migration Costs: Waiting until 2026-2027 to begin migration will result in a sharp increase in consulting rates (30-50% higher) due to a surge in demand and limited availability of experienced SAP resources

·Compressed Implementation Timelines & Business Disruptions: A rushed migration leads to higher risks of project delays, data inconsistencies, and operational disruptions, compared to a planned, phased approach.

·Missed Opportunity for Cost Optimization: Early adopters of S/4HANA can leverage automation, process standardization, and cloud efficiencies to reduce overall IT and operational costs. Late movers will continue to incur high legacy costs and miss out on business efficiencies.

Forward-thinking companies are already investing in AI-powered ERP transformation and reaping benefits in cost reduction, process efficiency, and real-time analytics. Organizations that wait until 2026-27 will face higher costs, increased project risks, and competitive disadvantages.

Key Opportunities of S/4HANA for Business Agility and Digital Transformation

Beyond the deadline, the real question organizations should be asking is: What value can S/4HANA unlock for my business today? The move to S/4HANA is not just about keeping systems running—it’s about creating a more agile, AI-driven, and cost-efficient enterprise. Companies that migrate early will benefit from Business Agility, AI-driven Innovation, Industry specific Business Transformation and will become Future ready.

1. Achieving Business Agility: S/4HANA as a Real-Time Enterprise Core

SAP ECC’s reliance on batch-based processing and siloed data structures creates decision-making bottlenecks across all business functions. Organizations today need real-time visibility, AI-driven insights, and automated business processes to remain agile and competitive.

i.CXO-Level Impact: S/4HANA provides a single, real-time financial and operational truth that enables faster, data-driven decision-making for CFOs, COOs, and CEOs. The ability to run real-time simulations and what-if analyses enables proactive business steering rather than reactive adjustments.

ii. Business Team Impact: Department heads in finance, procurement, supply chain, and HR can leverage embedded analytics, intelligent automation, and process optimization to improve performance. Finance teams can accelerate month-end closing, procurement teams can automate vendor selection, and supply chain managers can use AI-driven demand forecasting.

iii. Shop Floor & Operational Workforce Impact: Automated workflows in production, warehouse management, and logistics reduce manual work, streamline exception handling, and improve efficiency. Real-time operational dashboards ensure that plant managers and warehouse supervisors have the right insights at the right time.

2. Unlocking AI-Driven Innovation Across Enterprise Functions

S/4HANA natively integrates AI and automation, revolutionizing key business areas:

AI-Driven Innovation across Business Function

3. Industry-Specific Digital Transformation with S/4HANA

SAP S/4HANA is designed to support industry-specific business models, providing capabilities that go beyond traditional ERP functionality.

Industry-Specific Digital Transformation with S/4HANA

4. SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP): Extending Innovation Beyond ERP

SAP BTP enables organizations to extend their core ERP capabilities by integrating AI, automation, and third-party applications—but more importantly, it democratizes innovation across all levels of the organization.

i. SAP BTP enables agile innovation through AI-driven workflows, open APIs, and machine learning automation, allowing companies to scale transformation without heavy IT dependency.

ii. Low-code/no-code capabilities empower finance, HR, and procurement teams to automate repetitive processes without needing deep technical expertise, improving agility in business-driven automation.

iii. BTP acts as a bridge between legacy ECC applications and modern cloud-native solutions, ensuring that IT teams can manage integration without significant custom development efforts.

Recognizing the transformative potential of S/4HANA is only the first step. The next critical decision is choosing the right deployment model that aligns with business goals, industry demands, and IT strategy. Whether organizations opt for RISE with SAP, Private Cloud, or On-Premise, the choice must balance cost, flexibility, AI readiness, and long-term scalability

Selecting the Right Deployment Strategy: Beyond RISE with SAP

SAP provides multiple deployment options for businesses transitioning to S/4HANA, each with unique benefits. While RISE with SAP offers an all-in-one transformation package, many organizations prefer hybrid or on-premise strategies due to regulatory, operational, or financial considerations. The below table depicts the comparison of Deployment Options with AI Capabilities

Right Deployment Strategy: Beyond RISE with SAP

Each approach allows businesses to leverage AI, automation, and analytics while balancing operational needs and cost constraints.

Selecting a deployment path is only part of the journey. Successful S/4HANA adoption requires upfront preparation, including data cleansing, business process harmonization, and interface rationalization. Organizations that invest in these key prerequisites will significantly reduce implementation risks, accelerate go-live timelines, and maximize business value.

Preparing for S/4HANA: Key Business & IT Prerequisites

A well-structured pre-migration strategy is essential to accelerate time-to-value, optimize costs, and minimize operational risks. The implementation approach (Greenfield, Brownfield, or Bluefield) determines the scope of these prerequisites.

Key Pre-Migration Prerequisites Based on Approach

Pre-Migration Prerequisites By addressing these prerequisites before migration, or

By addressing these prerequisites before migration, organizations can shorten implementation timelines by 10-25% reducing risks and costs.

For businesses not yet ready to transition, SAP’s extended support options provide a temporary buffer—but at a cost. Whether considering extended ECC maintenance until 2030, leveraging RISE for partial transition, or exploring third-party support, organizations must carefully evaluate the financial, security, and innovation risks of delaying migration.

Extended Support Options: Navigating SAP’s Plan for Beyond 2027

As SAP phases out mainstream support for ECC, businesses must weigh the costs and risks of delaying migration versus the strategic advantages of early S/4HANA adoption. While SAP offers extended support until 2030 and RISE with SAP as a transition path, relying on these options without a clear migration roadmap can lead to higher costs, security vulnerabilities, and missed innovation opportunities.

Extended Support and Maintain Option

Strategic Takeaway: Future-Proof Your ERP, Don’t Just Extend Support

I. If staying on ECC: Businesses must have a defined transition plan, as relying on extended support is a short-term fix, not a future-proof strategy.

II. If delaying migration: Organizations should use RISE with SAP to minimize risks while preparing for full S/4HANA adoption.

III. If prioritizing transformation: Moving to S/4HANA now ensures access to AI, automation, and cost efficiencies before industry-wide demand surges.

Delaying the inevitable increases costs and risks—SAP’s extended support is a bridge, not a destination.

While extended support offers short-term relief, it is not a long-term solution. Businesses that act early on S/4HANA migration will gain cost advantages, accelerate AI adoption, and establish a foundation for future growth. The opportunity is clear—companies must move beyond legacy constraints and take a proactive approach to digital transformation before market pressure forces their hand.

Conclusion: Why Businesses Must Act Now

The shift to SAP S/4HANA is not just a system upgrade—it is a strategic move toward building a resilient, AI-driven enterprise. Organizations that embrace this transition early will gain a competitive edge in financial efficiency, supply chain agility, and operational intelligence, while those delaying risk higher costs, compliance challenges, and technology obsolescence.

With SAP focusing all future innovations on S/4HANA, businesses running ECC beyond 2027 will face increased maintenance costs, limited AI capabilities, and growing integration complexities. Migration is no longer an IT-driven decision—it is a C-suite priority to enable scalability, automation, and digital transformation.

Key Business Imperatives for Immediate Action-

I.Cost & Efficiency Gains: Eliminate legacy maintenance costs, optimize IT investments, and enhance operational efficiencies with cloud-enabled automation.

II. Regulatory Readiness & Risk Mitigation: Stay ahead of evolving ESG, tax, and financial compliance mandates with real-time reporting and built-in audit controls.

III. AI-Driven Business Agility: Enable predictive decision-making, intelligent automation, and enhanced supply chain visibility to respond dynamically to market shifts.

IV. Future-Proofed Digital Core: Leverage seamless cloud and AI integration to drive business model innovation and sustain long-term growth.

The window to transition is narrowing. Businesses that act now will not only secure cost advantages but also position themselves at the forefront of digital transformation. Waiting is not an option—the future of enterprise technology is here. So decide and act fast!!

entry.title: Migrate your data through Migration cockpit.
entry.content:
Dear Team,

 

We are currently utilizing the data migration cockpit template for BP vendor creation and have encountered an issue during the simulation phase. The error message displayed is: "Enter External Number" (Message no: CVIV_UI 6).
In our existing system, we follow a different number range concept, as outlined below:

 

Account Group: XXXX

 

BP Number Range: Internal    NR(XXXX)

 

Vendor Number Range: External NR(XXXX)

 

Additionally, there is no checkbox available for maintaining the same numbers.
Could you please advise whether the migration cockpit is designed to work exclusively with the same number range, or if it can accommodate different number ranges as well?Your guidance on this matter would be greatly appreciated.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Consumption in bill of material
entry.content:
Hi all,

could you please advise for following issue which we are facing. We are packing finished goods into trays, we need 2 trays for 10 pieces of finish goods(lets say top and bottom). In BOM we have set up base quantity 10 pieces, for mentioned raw material is component quantity 2 pieces.

Logic which is used in reality is like this:

  • Producing 1-10 pieces of finished goods use 2 pieces of raw material.
  • Producing 11-20 pieces of finished goods use 4 pieces of raw material.
  • Producing 21-30 pieces of finished goods use 6 pieces of raw material, and so on.

Issue is that SAP is not calculating "low quantities" correctly. When we are production 1-5 pieces, system calculate only 1 piece of tray, which is not correct - we still use 2 trays.

We cannot use fixed quantity because if we are producing for example 10-15pieces for which we use 4 trays(10 pieces FG =2 trays plus another 5 pieces FG= 2 trays), with fixed quantity it would be always 2.

Also create another alternative BOMs and then another production version based on lot size is not the correct way for us, there would be a lot of prod. versions.

Could you please advise if it is possible make setup which will reflect calculation above?

entry.title: How to create custom catalogs and target mapping
entry.content:
Is there any detailed steps on how we can create a custom catalog and group as well as the target mapping?

addition to our query, may we know how to delete the published variant in UI?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Unable to find linkage between PPDS buckets and sales order
entry.content:
Hi SAP,
We have a requirement where we need to change object status in sales order when a PP/DS Block is turned to "Fixed".

i.e when we open transaction /SAPAPO/CDPS0 and right click on a block, it gives option of block maintenance. When we click that we have and option to make the block "Fixed". When this happens we are using BADI /SAPAPO/IF_EX_CRESPPBUCKET~PPDSBUCKET_LAYOUT_CHANGE (please suggest better if possible) and post that we need to fetch sales order number and sales order item no. to change it"s status. Please Help!
